div.head: The Chargé in Austria (Wiley) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 4. Vienna Nazi leader Tavs arrested and National leader Leopold interrogated. Incriminating documents seized including plan drawn up by Tavs for Nazi uprising this year involving German participation. Foreign Office is inclined to regard plan as personal rather than officially approved project. Tavs’ private archives are now being deciphered for further enlightenment. Foreign Office is informed that NeurathBaron Konstantin von Neurath, Reich Foreign Minister. recently made alarming statements to François-PoncetAndré François-Poncet, French Ambassador in Germany. and FlandinPierre Etienne Flandin, leader of the opposition in the French Independent Radical Party. on the subject of German attitude toward Austrian independence. This is perhaps basic [basis of] rumors reported by Berlin in telegram 17 of January 18, noon.
